title: Location
desc: Parkland FL puts you between Miami and West Palm Beach — here's why that location advantage matters more than most buyers realize.
excerpt: When my family moved here from Los Angeles, one of the first things I noticed was how much ground you can cover without it feeling like a commitment. On a Saturday morning I can...
body:
Parkland, Florida's Location: Between Miami, West Palm Beach, and Everywhere in Between

April 18, 2026locationBy Alex Sverdlik

When my family moved here from Los Angeles, one of the first things I noticed was how much ground you can cover without it feeling like a commitment. On a Saturday morning I can be in Wynwood having coffee before most people in Parkland have finished breakfast. On a Sunday I can take the girls up to Palm Beach for lunch and be back well before dinner.

That kind of range matters more than people expect when they're shopping for a home.

Where Parkland Actually Sits on the Map

Parkland is in northern Broward County, just south of the Palm Beach County line. That puts it roughly 35 miles north of Miami and about 30 miles south of West Palm Beach — close enough to both that neither feels like a trip.

The Turnpike and I-95 both run close by, and once you know which entrance to use, the drives are straightforward. Fort Lauderdale is about 20 minutes south. Boca Raton is literally next door — I drive through it to get to the Turnpike.

Miami Is Closer Than It Sounds

Forty-five minutes on a weekend morning gets you into Brickell or Coconut Grove without any drama. Midweek, I'd leave a little earlier if you have a meeting downtown, but for dinners and events the timing is easy to manage.

The Design District, Wynwood, South Beach, Aventura — all of it is accessible in a way it simply isn't if you're living in, say, Jupiter or Wellington. For buyers who want a quieter residential base but don't want to give up access to a real city, this location is hard to argue with.

West Palm Beach and Palm Beach Are a Relaxed Drive North

Worth Avenue, the Norton Museum, the Kravis Center, Palm Beach International Airport — all of it is about 35 to 40 minutes up I-95 or the Turnpike. A lot of my clients fly in and out of PBI specifically because it's less chaotic than Fort Lauderdale-Hollywood, and the drive back to Parkland is clean.

For anyone who travels regularly for work, having two major airports within reach — FLL and PBI, with MIA not much farther — is a practical advantage that doesn't show up on any listing sheet.

Naples and the Gulf Coast Are a Day Trip, Not a Weekend Trip

This surprises people who aren't from Florida. Alligator Alley — that's I-75 running straight across the Everglades — puts Naples about two hours west of Parkland. It's a flat, easy drive. Marco Island is a bit farther south. Sanibel is farther north along the coast.

My family has done the Naples day trip more times than I can count. Leave at nine, spend the afternoon on Fifth Avenue South or out on the water, drive home. It doesn't require a hotel. That's a Florida-specific lifestyle benefit that people from the Northeast or California genuinely don't anticipate until they're living it.

Quiet Neighborhood, Connected Life

What makes this location work is that Parkland itself doesn't feel like a suburb of anything. The neighborhoods here — Heron Bay, MiraLago, Parkland Golf and Country Club, the ranches — have their own character and their own pace. You don't feel like you're living adjacent to somewhere better.

But when you want the city, the beach, the Gulf Coast, or a flight out — it's all within reach in a way that genuinely affects quality of life. If you're coming from New York or New Jersey, you're used to measuring everything by the tunnel and the bridge. Down here the math is different and, honestly, more forgiving.

For buyers thinking about the broader lifestyle picture — not just square footage and school ratings — Parkland's location is one of the things I find myself talking about in almost every conversation.

If you want to talk through how this area fits what you're looking for, reach out at ZenQuest Realty and we can have a real conversation about it.
